text: Setting Up Amazon Q Agents for Minimal Code Generation
label: general

text: To lighten the load when scaling the "Build your own..." projects, we'll create 11 Amazon Q Agents—one per category from the hierarchy (Foundational Builds, Language and Compilation Builds, etc.). This modular approach distributes the workload: Each agent focuses on generating minimal viable code (e.g., basic skeletons, core functions, and simple tests) for its category's projects, allowing parallel invocation or chaining without overwhelming a single agent. For example, you can run multiple agents concurrently in VS Code for different categories, or chain them via prompts for dependencies (e.g., foundational code feeding into OS/kernel).
label: tutorial

text: This setup uses Amazon Q's custom agent features (via CLI or console), leveraging Claude Sonnet 4 for efficient code outputs. Agents will produce starter code in languages like C++, Rust, or Python, with minimal implementations (e.g., a basic CPU emulator loop without full optimization). To scale:
label: tutorial

text: Parallel Use: Invoke agents independently for non-dependent categories (e.g., run Networking and Data Storage agents simultaneously).
label: data

text: Load Management: Limit to 11 agents to avoid complexity; if needed, group further (e.g., merge UI and Games into one), but 11 balances granularity and manageability.
label: graphics

text: Prerequisites: AWS account with Amazon Q Pro, VS Code with AWS Toolkit extension, and CLI installed (aws configure for credentials).
label: general

text: Via AWS CLI: Run aws q create-agent --name <agent-name> --configuration '<json-config>' for each.
label: tutorial

text: Via Console: Go to Amazon Q Developer > Agents > Create Custom Agent, paste JSON.
label: tutorial

text: Invocation: In VS Code Q Chat: @<agent-name> Generate minimal code for [project] in [language].
label: general

text: Scaling Tip: For bulk, use AWS Step Functions to orchestrate (e.g., trigger all agents on a schedule) or parallel queries in Q Chat.
label: general

text: Here are the 11 agent JSON configs. Each includes a prompt template focused on minimal code (e.g., core logic, no advanced features), with tools for code gen and testing.

label: general
